intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 13 — round-2 redesign for R1 cascade)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator EXCHANGES letters with a friend after class — the friend brings a short note from the classroom asking the narrator to read, the narrator reads it silently, then writes a long reply that makes the friend laugh.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the narrator is alone reading at school; at the closer the narrator and friend share a back-and-forth of letters and the friend is laughing at the narrator's reply. Companionship (alone → paired), object-flow (one short note in → one long reply back out), and emotional register (silent reading → laughter) all change.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a classroom / kitchen / rooftop story. Scene_class = school_after_class (general school grounds; the classroom is a SOURCE in s2, not the scene). First time the corpus has staged a peer letter-exchange.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 手紙 (letter). Last 5 anchors = 卵 / 本 / 鉛筆 / 雲 / 電話 — 手紙 not on the list. Five occurrences spanning s3, s4, s5, s6, s7 — given to narrator, contains a request, read silently, written-back, looked-at-and-laughed-at. Active causality at every step.
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: P:noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-WO-V-te-V (友達は私の手紙を見て、笑いました。). NOT a noun-pile, NOT noun-WA-noun-DESU (story 12 mirror), NOT noun-NO-noun-WO-noun-NI-noun-DESU (story 11), NOT noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-NI-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun (story 10). Two-verb closer (見て、笑いました) lands the friend's reaction.
  why_not_filler:        narrator (the receiver-then-replier); friend (the giver-then-reactor — gets a real entrance at s1, brings the artefact at s3, smiles at the reply at s7); 手紙 (anchor — given, contains a request, read, replied-to, looked-at); 教室 (the SOURCE the friend just left — explains why the friend has the letter and the narrator does not); 学校 + 本 (the why-here: narrator was already alone-and-reading when the friend arrived); 短い + 長い (paired contrast: short note in, long reply out — the size asymmetry is the relational beat).
  obligations_absorbed:  must-introduce N5_te_kudasai via the letter's content 「読んでください。」 at s4 — a textbook-canonical first appearance (the request is what makes the letter act on the narrator); must-reinforce N5_soshite via s6 「そして、長い手紙を書きました。」 (organic — links reading the request to writing the reply). R1 word reinforcements (forced by post-ship pytest sweep that flagged story 12's W00084-88 with 0 follow-ups in story 13): W00084 短い @ s3, W00085 教室 @ s2, W00087 長い @ s6, W00088 くれる @ s3 — ALL FOUR story-12 mints reinforced organically by the letter-exchange premise. R1 cleared.

GRAMMAR DEFERRAL: brief recommended N4_passive (priority 6). Deferred — passive forces unnatural pedagogical phrasing in a week-4 N5 reader, and the brief notes 21 N5 points are still uncovered (Check 3.9 will block any tier advancement until N5 is cleared). Chose N5_te_kudasai (polite request, foundational paradigm anchor) instead — lands cleanly inside the letter's quoted content.

LEXICAL: 3 mints — 手紙 (letter), 静か (quiet/silent — used adverbially as 静かに at s5), 笑う (to laugh). All N5 / top-frequency. No overrides used.

ROUND-1 ABANDONED: Initial draft was a station scene (umbrella shelter offered to a stranger). Gauntlet was clean and §E.7/E.7.5 passed, but post-ship pytest flagged R1 violation: story 12's four mints (W00084 短い, W00085 教室, W00087 長い, W00088 くれる) had zero follow-up in story 13 and the available-followups window was 1, requiring all four to land in story 13. State backups restored; story 13 re-designed as a peer letter-exchange that absorbs all four naturally.

OVERRIDE USED (1/1): §E.7-redesign returned REWRITE-STORY citing structural mirror with story 12 ('giver→object arrival→narrator response→giver reaction'). Override justified by concrete error in reviewer reasoning: stories 11/12 are HIERARCHICAL ONE-DIRECTIONAL transfers (mother gives, teacher gives) whereas story 13 is a PEER BIDIRECTIONAL EXCHANGE (s3 narrator receives + s6 narrator sends). The bidirectional reciprocity at peer register IS structurally novel; the reviewer conflated 'character A gives to character B' with the closer-shape-of-reaction pattern, missing that s3 + s6 together form a back-and-forth no prior story has had. §E.7.5-redesign returned REWRITE-SENTENCE; all 3 fixes (s1 dropped 私のところに, s2 swapped は→が, s4 swapped 「と書きました」→「とありました」) applied and verified.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3): classroom, kitchen, rooftop
  anchor_object  (last 5): 卵, 本, 鉛筆, 雲, 電話
  closer_shape   (last 3): noun-NO-noun-WO-noun-NI-noun-DESU, noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-NI-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun, noun-WA-noun-DESU
  opening_seqs   (last 3): 夕方/母/私, 朝/台所/行きます, 私/鉛筆/短かった

sentences.jp: 学校で本を読みました。
sentences.en: I was reading a book at school.

sentences.jp: 友達が教室から来ました。
sentences.en: My friend came from the classroom.

sentences.jp: 友達は私に短い手紙をくれました。
sentences.en: My friend gave me a short letter.

sentences.jp: 手紙に「読んでください。」とありました。
sentences.en: On the letter it said, "Please read."

sentences.jp: 私は静かに手紙を読みました。
そして、長い手紙を書きました。
sentences.en: And then I wrote a long letter.

sentences.jp: 友達は私の手紙を見て、笑いました。
sentences.en: My friend looked at my letter and laughed.
